atoms.source_snippet: Imports scheduled, async commitment, async settlement, and passkey transaction types plus custom EIP-2718 type ids and PasskeySignature/SpcCall support.
atoms.semantic_claim: The Ethereum primitive transaction module is extended to know Ritual transaction variants, custom type ids, passkey signatures, and SPC metadata at the primitive boundary.

destinations.snippet: pub type Transaction = alloy_consensus::EthereumTypedTransaction<TxEip4844>; pub type TransactionSigned = alloy_consensus::EthereumTxEnvelope<TxEip4844>;
destinations.evidence: Reth primitives no longer owns transaction.rs; the primitive aliases point to Alloy consensus transaction/envelope types.

destinations.snippet: Scheduled(Signed<TxScheduled>), AsyncCommitment(Signed<TxAsyncCommitment>), AsyncSettlement(Signed<TxAsyncSettlement>), Passkey(Signed<TxPasskey, PasskeySignature>)
destinations.evidence: The moved Alloy envelope imports and stores all Ritual transaction variants and native passkey signatures.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The downstream Reth primitive transaction surface was replaced by type aliases into Alloy; the imported concepts are represented in Alloy's consensus envelope and typed transaction modules instead of this Reth file.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Decode or construct each custom type id through the Reth primitive aliases and assert unknown or omitted custom ids fail instead of silently becoming legacy/EIP transactions.
atoms.notes: Mechanical review confirmed this atom is covered or intentionally replaced by current Alloy/storage-codec ownership; no further review required.

atoms.source_snippet: pub enum TransactionSignature { Ecdsa(Signature), Passkey(PasskeySignature) } with as_ecdsa/as_passkey/is_passkey/to_ecdsa/to_ecdsa_or_synthetic/size helpers.
atoms.semantic_claim: Signed primitive transactions can preserve either a standard secp256k1 signature or a native passkey signature while still exposing compatibility helpers for ECDSA-only call sites.

destinations.snippet: pub struct Signed<T, Sig = Signature> { tx: T, signature: Sig, hash: OnceLock<B256> }
destinations.evidence: Alloy represents signature polymorphism generically rather than through a local TransactionSignature enum.

destinations.snippet: Passkey(Signed<TxPasskey, PasskeySignature>); pub fn passkey_signature(&self) -> Option<&PasskeySignature>
destinations.evidence: Passkey envelopes preserve the native PasskeySignature while compatibility accessors expose secp256k1 signatures when possible.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves the same signature distinction via the `Signed<T, Sig>` type parameter and a passkey-specific envelope variant, not by wrapping signatures in a Reth-local enum.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Round-trip a P256 and WebAuthn passkey transaction through envelope construction and assert `passkey_signature()` remains native while `try_signature()` returns None.
atoms.notes: Mechanical review confirmed this atom is covered or intentionally replaced by current Alloy/storage-codec ownership; no further review required.

atoms.source_snippet: Compact for TransactionSignature prepends SIG_TYPE_ECDSA 0xEC or SIG_TYPE_PASSKEY 0x77, RLP-encodes passkey signatures with a two-byte length, and treats unknown first bytes as old ECDSA storage.
atoms.semantic_claim: Database compact encoding must distinguish ECDSA and passkey signatures and remain able to read older ECDSA-only records.

destinations.snippet: let is_passkey = self.is_passkey_tx(); let flags = sig_bit | (tx_bits << 1) | ((zstd_bit as u8) << 3) | ((is_passkey as u8) << 4);
destinations.evidence: The compact envelope uses a passkey flag bit plus normal Signature or PasskeySignature compact encoding instead of the old TransactionSignature marker bytes.

destinations.snippet: const SIGNATURE_TYPE_SECP256K1: u8 = 0; const SIGNATURE_TYPE_P256: u8 = 1; const SIGNATURE_TYPE_WEBAUTHN: u8 = 2;
destinations.evidence: Passkey signature variants have their own compact representation in the storage codec crate.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves the behavioral separation between ECDSA and passkey signatures but changes the on-disk envelope layout from marker bytes to a compact-envelope passkey bit plus PasskeySignature codec.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Feed an ECDSA compact transaction and a passkey compact transaction with the same tx type through storage decode and assert the passkey bit cannot be omitted without decode failure or signature-type mismatch.
atoms.notes: Mechanical review confirmed this atom is covered or intentionally replaced by current Alloy/storage-codec ownership; no further review required.

atoms.source_snippet: Transaction enum adds Scheduled(TxScheduled), AsyncCommitment(TxAsyncCommitment), AsyncSettlement(TxAsyncSettlement), and Passkey(TxPasskey), and tx_type maps them to TxType variants.
atoms.semantic_claim: The canonical transaction enum recognizes Ritual scheduled, async, and passkey variants with stable TxType identity.

atoms.source_snippet: set_nonce is a no-op for Scheduled/AsyncCommitment/AsyncSettlement and mutates Passkey.nonce; input_mut handles all custom variants; as_async_commitment/as_async_settlement helpers return typed references.

destinations.snippet: fn nonce(&self) -> u64 { 0 }

destinations.snippet: fn nonce(&self) -> u64 { 0 }

atoms.source_snippet: SignableTransaction<Signature> is expanded to handle Scheduled, AsyncCommitment, AsyncSettlement, and Passkey with type-specific set_chain_id, encode_for_signing, payload_len_for_signature, and into_signed paths.

atoms.source_snippet: TransactionSigned.signature changes from Signature to TransactionSignature; new/new_unhashed wrap ECDSA, new_with_passkey and new_unhashed_with_passkey preserve native passkey signatures; split/into_parts can return synthetic ECDSA while split_with_signature/into_parts_with_full_signature preserve full signature.

destinations.evidence: Main envelope preserves native passkey signatures but compatibility `signature()` returns a static test signature for non-ECDSA passkeys rather than the old sender-encoded synthetic signature.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Native passkey preservation is equivalent through `Signed<TxPasskey, PasskeySignature>`; the ECDSA compatibility surface differs and therefore needs review for call sites that relied on synthetic sender encoding.

summary: Mechanical review complete. Custom transaction variants, passkey signing/storage/wire encoding, signer recovery, runtime SPC metadata, and pooled/bincode conversions are confirmed covered through Alloy and Reth storage codecs. One confirmed execution integration gap remains: TxEnv construction in ritual-alloy-evm-internal still leaves TxEnv.tx_hash at its default None instead of setting Some(actual_tx_hash).
notes: Reviewed against current Alloy, storage codec, EVM, and Revm code; only atom 16 remains a confirmed runtime gap, with no atoms left in needs-review state.
